revision.comment: moved [[Kashihara-jingu]] to [[Kashihara Shrine]]: [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style (Japan-related articles)#Temples and shrines]]
Various small edits including English name, Japanese pronunciation, link update, city and prefecture, and updates to external links

revision.text:
[[Image:Kashihara M6485.jpg|thumb|Outer prayer hall]] [[Image:Kashihara M6522.jpg|thumb|Inner prayer hall]] The {{nihongo|'''Kashihara Shrine'''|橿原神宮|Kashihara Jingū|}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Kashihara Shrine was built in [[1889]] at the site of the Kashihara-gū where Japan's first emperor, [[Emperor Jimmu]], is said to have acceded to the throne.
==External links== *[http://www.kashiharajingu-wedding.jp/kashijin/index.html Official Site] (Japanese) *[http://www.jnto.go.jp/eng/arrange/attractions/facilities/shrines_temples/83dn3a000000ej5u.html [[Japan National Tourist Organization]] Site]
{{japan-struct-stub}} {{reli-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}}

[[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 2nd, 1890 | date_destroyed = | location = 934 Kume-cho, [[Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara-shi]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | website = {{URL|http://www.kashiharajingu.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = Kigensai (February 11th) | leadership = }} The {{Nihongo|'''Kashihara Shrine'''|橿原神宮|Kashihara Jingū}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine was built in 2 April [[1890]] at the site of the Kashihara-gū, which is situated on the southeastern side of [[Mount Unebi]] of [[Yamato Sanzan]]. Japan's first Emperor, [[Emperor Jimmu]], is said to have acceded to the throne on [[National Foundation Day (Japan)|11 February]] 660 BC at the Kashihara-gū. ==Access== *a 10-minute walk from the center gate of [[Kashiharajingu-mae Station]] on [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] Lines ([[Minami Osaka Line]], [[Yoshino Line]], [[Kashihara Line]]s). ==Gallery== File:Kashihara M6522.jpg|Inner prayer hall, with unusually placed [[Chigi (architecture)|''chigi'']] File:Kashihara-jingū,_Ge-haiden_01.jpg [[File:Tomb_of_Emperor_Jimmu,_haisho.JPG|right|thumb|''Unebi Goryō'', the mausoleum of Jimmu in [[Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]]] ==Note== {{Reflist}} ==See also== *[[List of Jingū]] *[[The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture]] ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Kashihara-jingū|Kashihara Shrine}} *[http://www.kashiharajingu.or.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Jingū]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Chokusaisha]] {{japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}}

[[Chokusaisha]]
[[Beppyo jinja]] | deity = [[Emperor Jimmu]]
[[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 2nd, 1890 | date_destroyed = | location = 934 Kume-cho, [[Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara-shi]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | website = {{URL|http://www.kashiharajingu.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = Kigensai (February 11th) | leadership = }} The {{Nihongo|'''Kashihara Shrine'''|橿原神宮|Kashihara Jingū}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine was built in 2 April [[1890]] at the site of the Kashihara-gū, which is situated on the southeastern side of [[Mount Unebi]] of [[Yamato Sanzan]]. Japan's first Emperor, [[Emperor Jimmu]], is said to have acceded to the throne on [[National Foundation Day (Japan)|11 February]] 660 BC at the Kashihara-gū. The Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu is found on the shrine's premises. ==Access== *a 10-minute walk from the center gate of [[Kashiharajingu-mae Station]] on [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] Lines ([[Minami Osaka Line]], [[Yoshino Line]], [[Kashihara Line]]s). ==Gallery== File:Kashihara M6522.jpg|Inner prayer hall, with unusually placed [[Chigi (architecture)|''chigi'']] 橿原神宮 | image = Kashihara M6485.jpg | caption = Outer prayer hall | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34.48843|N|135.786453|E|format=dms|region:JP_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Kanpeitaisha]]
[[Chokusaisha]]
[[Beppyo jinja]] | deity = [[Emperor Jimmu]]

The shrine was built in 2 April [[1890]] at the site of the Kashihara-gū, which is situated on the southeastern side of [[Mount Unebi]] of [[Yamato Sanzan]]. Japan's first Emperor, [[Emperor Jimmu]], is said to have acceded to the throne on [[National Foundation Day (Japan)|11 February]] 660 BC at the Kashihara-gū. The Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu is found on the shrine's premises. ==Access== *a 10-minute walk from the center gate of [[Kashiharajingu-mae Station]] on [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] Lines ([[Minami Osaka Line]], [[Yoshino Line]], [[Kashihara Line]]s). ==Gallery== File:Kashihara M6522.jpg|Inner prayer hall, with unusually placed [[Chigi (architecture)|''chigi'']] {{short description|Shinto shrine in Kashihara, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kashihara Shrine
橿原神宮 | image = Kashihara M6485.jpg | caption = Outer prayer hall | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34.48843|N|135.786453|E|format=dms|region:JP_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Kanpeitaisha]]
[[Chokusaisha]]
[[Beppyo jinja]] | deity = [[Emperor Jimmu]]
[[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 2nd, 1890 | date_destroyed = | location = 934 Kume-cho, [[Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara-shi]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | website = {{URL|http://www.kashiharajingu.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = Kigensai (February 11th) | leadership = }} The {{Nihongo|'''Kashihara Shrine'''|橿原神宮|Kashihara Jingū}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine was built in 2 April [[1890]] at the site of the Kashihara-gū, which is situated on the southeastern side of [[Mount Unebi]] of [[Yamato Sanzan]]. Japan's first Emperor, [[Emperor Jimmu]], is said to have acceded to the throne on [[National Foundation Day (Japan)|11 February]] 660 BC at the Kashihara-gū. The Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu is found on the shrine's premises. ==Access== *a 10-minute walk from the center gate of [[Kashiharajingu-mae Station]] on [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] Lines ([[Minami Osaka Line]], [[Yoshino Line]], [[Kashihara Line]]s). ==Gallery== File:Kashihara M6522.jpg|Inner prayer hall, with unusually placed [[Chigi (architecture)|''chigi'']] File:Kashihara-jingū,_Ge-haiden_01.jpg File:Tomb_of_Emperor_Jimmu,_haisho.JPG|''Unebi Goryō'', the mausoleum of Jimmu in [[Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]] ==Note== {{Reflist}} {{short description|Shinto shrine in Kashihara, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kashihara Shrine
橿原神宮 | image = Kashihara M6485.jpg | caption = Outer prayer hall | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34.48843|N|135.786453|E|format=dms|region:JP_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Kanpeitaisha]]
[[Chokusaisha]]
[[Beppyo jinja]] | deity = [[Emperor Jimmu]]
[[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 2nd, 1890 | date_destroyed = | location = 934 Kume-cho, [[Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara-shi]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | website = {{URL|http://www.kashiharajingu.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = Kigensai (February 11th) | leadership = }} The {{Nihongo|'''Kashihara Shrine'''|橿原神宮|Kashihara Jingū}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine was built in 2 April [[1890]] at the site of the Kashihara-gū, which is situated on the southeastern side of [[Mount Unebi]] of [[Yamato Sanzan]]. Japan's first Emperor, [[Emperor Jimmu]], is said to have acceded to the throne on [[National Foundation Day (Japan)|11 February]] 660 BC at the Kashihara-gū. The Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu is found on the shrine's premises. ==Access== *a 10-minute walk from the center gate of [[Kashiharajingu-mae Station]] on [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] Lines ([[Minami Osaka Line]], [[Yoshino Line]], [[Kashihara Line]]s). ==Gallery== File:Kashihara M6522.jpg|Inner prayer hall, with unusually placed [[Chigi (architecture)|''chigi'']] File:Kashihara-jingū,_Ge-haiden_01.jpg File:Tomb_of_Emperor_Jimmu,_haisho.JPG|''Unebi Goryō'', the mausoleum of Jimmu in [[Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]] ==Note== {{Reflist}} {{short description|Shinto shrine in Kashihara, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kashihara Shrine
橿原神宮 | image = Kashihara M6485.jpg | caption = Outer prayer hall | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34.48843|N|135.786453|E|format=dms|region:JP_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Kanpeitaisha]]
[[Chokusaisha]]
[[Beppyo jinja]] | deity = [[Emperor Jimmu]]
[[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 2nd, 1890 | date_destroyed = | location = 934 Kume-cho, [[Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara-shi]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | website = {{URL|http://www.kashiharajingu.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = Kigensai (February 11th) | leadership = }} The {{Nihongo|'''Kashihara Shrine'''|橿原神宮|Kashihara Jingū}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine was built in 2 April [[1890]] at the site of the Kashihara-gū, which is situated on the southeastern side of [[Mount Unebi]] of [[Yamato Sanzan]]. Japan's first Emperor, [[Emperor Jimmu]], is said to have acceded to the throne on [[National Foundation Day (Japan)|11 February]] 660 BC at the Kashihara-gū. The Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu is found on the shrine's premises. ==Access== *a 10-minute walk from the center gate of [[Kashiharajingu-mae Station]] on [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] Lines ([[Minami Osaka Line]], [[Yoshino Line]], [[Kashihara Line]]s). ==Gallery== File:Kashihara M6522.jpg|Inner prayer hall, with unusually placed [[Chigi (architecture)|''chigi'']] File:Kashihara-jingū,_Ge-haiden_01.jpg File:Tomb_of_Emperor_Jimmu,_haisho.JPG|''Unebi Goryō'', the mausoleum of Jimmu in [[Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]] ==Note== {{Reflist}} {{short description|Shinto shrine in Kashihara, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kashihara Shrine
橿原神宮 | image = Kashihara M6485.jpg | caption = Outer prayer hall | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34.48843|N|135.786453|E|format=dms|region:JP_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Kanpeitaisha]]
[[Chokusaisha]]
[[Beppyo jinja]] | deity = [[Emperor Jimmu]]
[[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 2nd, 1890 | date_destroyed = | location = 934 Kume-cho, [[Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara-shi]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | website = {{URL|http://www.kashiharajingu.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = Kigensai (February 11th) | leadership = }} The {{Nihongo|'''Kashihara Shrine'''|橿原神宮|Kashihara Jingū}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine was built in 2 April [[1890]] at the site of the Kashihara-gū, which is situated on the southeastern side of [[Mount Unebi]] of [[Yamato Sanzan]]. Japan's first Emperor, [[Emperor Jimmu]], is said to have acceded to the throne on [[National Foundation Day (Japan)|11 February]] 660 BC at the Kashihara-gū. The Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu is found on the shrine's premises. ==Access== *a 10-minute walk from the center gate of [[Kashiharajingu-mae Station]] on [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] Lines ([[Minami Osaka Line]], [[Yoshino Line]], [[Kashihara Line]]s). ==Gallery== File:Kashihara M6522.jpg|Inner prayer hall, with unusually placed [[Chigi (architecture)|''chigi'']] File:Kashihara-jingū,_Ge-haiden_01.jpg File:Tomb_of_Emperor_Jimmu,_haisho.JPG|''Unebi Goryō'', the mausoleum of Jimmu in [[Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]] ==Note== {{Reflist}} {{short description|Shinto shrine in Kashihara, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kashihara Shrine
橿原神宮 | image = Kashihara M6485.jpg | caption = Outer prayer hall | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34.48843|N|135.786453|E|format=dms|region:JP_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Kanpeitaisha]]
[[Chokusaisha]]
[[Beppyo jinja]] | deity = [[Emperor Jimmu]]
[[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 2nd, 1890 | date_destroyed = | location = 934 Kume-cho, [[Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara-shi]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | website = {{URL|http://www.kashiharajingu.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = Kigensai (February 11th) | leadership = }} The {{Nihongo|'''Kashihara Shrine'''|橿原神宮|Kashihara Jingū}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine was built in 2 April [[1890]] at the site of the Kashihara-gū, which is situated on the southeastern side of [[Mount Unebi]] of [[Yamato Sanzan]]. Japan's first Emperor, [[Emperor Jimmu]], is said to have acceded to the throne on [[National Foundation Day (Japan)|11 February]] 660 BC at the Kashihara-gū. The Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu is found on the shrine's premises. ==Access== *a 10-minute walk from the center gate of [[Kashiharajingu-mae Station]] on [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] Lines ([[Minami Osaka Line]], [[Yoshino Line]], [[Kashihara Line]]s). ==Gallery== File:Kashihara M6522.jpg|Inner prayer hall, with unusually placed [[Chigi (architecture)|''chigi'']] File:Kashihara-jingū,_Ge-haiden_01.jpg File:Tomb_of_Emperor_Jimmu,_haisho.JPG|''Unebi Goryō'', the mausoleum of Jimmu in [[Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]] ==Note== {{Reflist}} {{short description|Shinto shrine in Kashihara, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kashihara Shrine
橿原神宮 | image = Kashihara M6485.jpg | caption = Outer prayer hall | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34.48843|N|135.786453|E|format=dms|region:JP_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Kanpeitaisha]]
[[Chokusaisha]]
[[Beppyo jinja]] | deity = [[Emperor Jimmu]]
[[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 2nd, 1890 | date_destroyed = | location = 934 Kume-cho, [[Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara-shi]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | website = {{URL|http://www.kashiharajingu.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = Kigensai (February 11th) | leadership = }} The {{Nihongo|'''Kashihara Shrine'''|橿原神宮|Kashihara Jingū}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine was built in 2 April [[1890]] at the site of the Kashihara-gū, which is situated on the southeastern side of [[Mount Unebi]] of [[Yamato Sanzan]]. Japan's first Emperor, [[Emperor Jimmu]], is said to have acceded to the throne on [[National Foundation Day (Japan)|11 February]] 660 BC at the Kashihara-gū. The Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu is found on the shrine's premises. ==Access== *a 10-minute walk from the center gate of [[Kashiharajingu-mae Station]] on [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] Lines ([[Minami Osaka Line]], [[Yoshino Line]], [[Kashihara Line]]s). ==Gallery== File:Kashihara M6522.jpg|Inner prayer hall, with unusually placed [[Chigi (architecture)|''chigi'']] File:Kashihara-jingū,_Ge-haiden_01.jpg File:Tomb_of_Emperor_Jimmu,_haisho.JPG|''Unebi Goryō'', the mausoleum of Jimmu in [[Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]] ==Note== {{Reflist}} {{short description|Shinto shrine in Kashihara, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kashihara Shrine
橿原神宮 | image = Kashihara M6485.jpg | caption = Outer prayer hall | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34.48843|N|135.786453|E|format=dms|region:JP_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Kanpeitaisha]]
[[Chokusaisha]]
[[Beppyo jinja]] | deity = [[Emperor Jimmu]]
[[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 2nd, 1890 | date_destroyed = | location = 934 Kume-cho, [[Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara-shi]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | website = {{URL|http://www.kashiharajingu.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = Kigensai (February 11th) | leadership = }} The {{Nihongo|'''Kashihara Shrine'''|橿原神宮|Kashihara Jingū}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine was built in 2 April [[1890]] at the site of the Kashihara-gū, which is situated on the southeastern side of [[Mount Unebi]] of [[Yamato Sanzan]]. Japan's first Emperor, [[Emperor Jimmu]], is said to have acceded to the throne on [[National Foundation Day (Japan)|11 February]] 660 BC at the Kashihara-gū. The Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu is found on the shrine's premises. ==Access== *a 10-minute walk from the center gate of [[Kashiharajingu-mae Station]] on [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] Lines ([[Minami Osaka Line]], [[Yoshino Line]], [[Kashihara Line]]s). ==Gallery== File:Kashihara M6522.jpg|Inner prayer hall, with unusually placed [[Chigi (architecture)|''chigi'']] File:Kashihara-jingū,_Ge-haiden_01.jpg File:Tomb_of_Emperor_Jimmu,_haisho.JPG|''Unebi Goryō'', the mausoleum of Jimmu in [[Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]] ==Note== {{Reflist}} ==See also== *[[List of Jingū]] *[[The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture]] ==External links== {{Commons Category:11]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] {{short description|Shinto shrine in Kashihara, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kashihara Shrine
橿原神宮 | image = Kashihara M6485.jpg | caption = Outer prayer hall | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34.48843|N|135.786453|E|format=dms|region:JP_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Kanpeitaisha]]
[[Chokusaisha]]
[[Beppyo jinja]] | deity = [[Emperor Jimmu]]
[[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 2nd, 1890 | date_destroyed = | location = 934 Kume-cho, [[Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara-shi]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | website = {{URL|http://www.kashiharajingu.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = Kigensai (February 11th) | leadership = }} The {{Nihongo|'''Kashihara Shrine'''|橿原神宮|Kashihara Jingū}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine was built in 2 April [[1890]] at the site of the Kashihara-gū, which is situated on the southeastern side of [[Mount Unebi]] of [[Yamato Sanzan]]. Japan's first Emperor, [[Emperor Jimmu]], is said to have acceded to the throne on [[National Foundation Day (Japan)|11 February]] 660 BC at the Kashihara-gū. The Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu is found on the shrine's premises. ==Access== *a 10-minute walk from the center gate of [[Kashiharajingu-mae Station]] on [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] Lines ([[Minami Osaka Line]], [[Yoshino Line]], [[Kashihara Line]]s). ==Gallery== File:Kashihara M6522.jpg|Inner prayer hall, with unusually placed [[Chigi (architecture)|''chigi'']] File:Kashihara-jingū,_Ge-haiden_01.jpg File:Tomb_of_Emperor_Jimmu,_haisho.JPG|''Unebi Goryō'', the mausoleum of Jimmu in [[Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]] ==Note== {{Reflist}} ==See also== *[[List of Jingū]] *[[The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture]] ==External links== {{Commons Category:11]] [[Category-inline|Kashihara-jingū|Kashihara Shrine}} *[http://www.kashiharajingu.or.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{Shinto shrine}} {{Contemporary Jingu}} {{japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Jingū]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Chokusaisha]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:11 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kashihara Shrine]] [[en:Kashihara Shrine]] [[en:Kashihara Jingu]]

[[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] | founded_by = [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 2nd, 1890 | date_destroyed = | location = 934 Kume-cho, [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara-shi]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan | website = {{URL|http://www.kashiharajingu.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = Kigensai (February 11th) | leadership = }} The {{Nihongo|'''Kashihara Shrine'''|橿原神宮|Kashihara Jingū}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The shrine was built in 2 April [[:en:1890|1890]] at the site of the Kashihara-gū, which is situated on the southeastern side of [[:en:Mount Unebi|Mount Unebi]] of [[:en:Yamato Sanzan|Yamato Sanzan]]. 橿原神宮 | image = Kashihara M6485.jpg | caption = Outer prayer hall | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34.48843|N|135.786453|E|format=dms|region:JP_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpeitaisha]]
[[Chokusaisha]]
[[Beppyo Shrine]] | deity = {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}
[[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] | founded_by = {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|WD=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji|1=Emperor Meiji}} | established = April 2nd, 1890 | date_destroyed = | location = 934 Kume-cho, {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara-shi|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan | website = {{URL|http://www.kashiharajingu.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = Kigensai (February 11th) | leadership = }} The {{Nihongo|'''Kashihara Shrine'''|橿原神宮|Kashihara Jingū}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the city of {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The shrine was built in 2 April {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890|1=1890}} at the site of the Kashihara-gū, which is situated on the southeastern side of {{ill|Mount Unebi|en|Mount Unebi|WD=Q17606448|lt=Mount Unebi|1=Mount Unebi}} of {{ill|Yamato Sanzan|en|Yamato Sanzan|WD=Q1470150|lt=Yamato Sanzan|1=Yamato Sanzan}}. Japan's first Emperor, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, is said to have acceded to the throne on {{ill|National Foundation Day (Japan)|en|National Foundation Day (Japan)|WD=Q1059995|lt=11 February|1=National Foundation Day (Japan)}} 660 BC at the Kashihara-gū. The Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu is found on the shrine's premises. ==Access== *a 10-minute walk from the center gate of [[:en:Kashiharajingu-mae Station|Kashiharajingu-mae Station]] on {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} Lines ({{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Minami Osaka Line|1=Minami Osaka Line}}, {{ill|Yoshino Line|en|Yoshino Line|WD=Q901085|lt=Yoshino Line|1=Yoshino Line}}, {{ill|Kashihara Line|en|Kashihara Line|WD=Q2491066|lt=Kashihara Line|1=Kashihara Line}}s). ==Gallery== File:Kashihara M6522.jpg|Inner prayer hall, with unusually placed {{ill|Chigi (architecture)|en|Chigi (architecture)|WD=Q262282|lt=''chigi''|1=Chigi (architecture)}} File:Kashihara-jingū,_Ge-haiden_01.jpg File:Tomb_of_Emperor_Jimmu,_haisho.JPG|''Unebi Goryō'', the mausoleum of Jimmu in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara City|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} ==Note== {{Reflist}} ==See also== *{{ill|List of Jingū|en|List of Jingū|WD=Q120680118|lt=List of Jingū|1=List of Jingū}}
